short_description: Reduces noise while preserving spectral features
long_description:
Smoothing reduces high-frequency noise in spectroscopic data while preserving important spectral features and peak information.

Smoothing Methods:

1. Moving Average
   - Simple averaging over a sliding window
   - Fast and straightforward
   - Can flatten sharp peaks if window is too large
   - Good for initial noise reduction

2. Savitzky-Golay Filter
   - Polynomial-preserving filter
   - Better at maintaining spectral peak shapes
   - Uses polynomial fitting within sliding window
   - More computationally intensive than moving average
   - Requires polynomial order < window size

Parameters:

Window Size:
- Larger windows = stronger smoothing but more feature loss
- Typical values: 5-15 points
- Must be odd number
- Should be smaller than spectral feature width

Polynomial Order (Savitzky-Golay only):
- Order 2: parabolic fitting (recommended)
- Order 3-5: higher-degree polynomials
- Lower orders preserve features better

Multiway Data:
- Specify dimensionality for 3D+ data
- Direction parameter controls axis of smoothing

Tip: For best results, apply baseline correction before smoothing.

layout.name: method
layout.label: Smoothing Method
layout.tooltip: Moving Average: simple averaging. Savitzky-Golay: polynomial-preserving filter

layout.name: direction
layout.label: Direction (for multiway)
layout.tooltip: Dimension to smooth (-1 for default/last dimension)

layout.name: window_size
layout.label: Window Size
layout.tooltip: Number of points for moving average or Savitzky-Golay window

layout.name: polyorder
layout.label: Polynomial Order
layout.tooltip: Polynomial degree (must be smaller than the window size and typically 2-5)

layout.name: nway_flag
layout.label: Data Dimensionality
layout.tooltip: Automatically inherited from Load Data function

sections.short_description: Displays the input calibration spectra before smoothing.
sections.long_description: Shows the original X calibration data before smoothing is applied. Use this section to inspect noise levels and spectral features prior to filtering.
Shows the X calibration data after smoothing is applied. Compare against the original to verify that noise is reduced while spectral features are preserved.

sections.short_description: Contour view of one selected calibration sample before smoothing.
sections.long_description: Displays a contour plot of the original multiway calibration data for the selected sample index. Use the sample navigation control to inspect spatial/spectral structure prior to smoothing.

sections.short_description: Contour view of one selected calibration sample after smoothing.
sections.long_description: Displays a contour plot of the smoothed multiway calibration data for the selected sample index. Compare with the original contour to assess the effect of smoothing on signal distribution.

sections.short_description: Heatmap view of higher-dimensional calibration data before smoothing.
sections.long_description: Displays the original calibration data as a heatmap for n-way datasets with dimensionality greater than 2. Use slicing and axis controls to inspect specific samples before smoothing.

sections.short_description: Heatmap view of higher-dimensional calibration data after smoothing.
sections.long_description: Displays the smoothed calibration data as a heatmap for n-way datasets with dimensionality greater than 2. Compare with the original heatmap to validate the smoothing effect across slices and dimensions.

sections.short_description: Displays the input validation spectra before smoothing.
sections.long_description: Shows the original X validation data before smoothing is applied. Use this section to inspect noise levels and spectral features prior to filtering.
Shows the X validation data after smoothing is applied. Compare against the original to verify that noise is reduced while spectral features are preserved.

sections.long_description: Displays a contour plot of the original multiway validation data for the selected sample index.

sections.short_description: Contour view of one selected validation sample after smoothing.

sections.long_description: Displays the original validation data as a heatmap for n-way datasets with dimensionality greater than 2.
